front: What is the orthogonal projection of a vector onto another vector?
back: The orthogonal projection of vector u onto vector v is given by proj_v(u) = ( (u•v) / (v•v) ) * v. It is the component of u that lies in the direction of v.

front: What is the formula for the projection of a vector onto a subspace W with an orthonormal basis?
back: If {u₁, u₂, ..., uₖ} is an orthonormal basis for subspace W, the projection of a vector v onto W is: proj_W(v) = (v•u₁)u₁ + (v•u₂)u₂ + ... + (v•uₖ)uₖ.

front: What key property defines an orthogonal projection in an inner product space?
back: The vector (v - proj_W(v)) is orthogonal to every vector in the subspace W. This is often called the residual or error vector being orthogonal to the subspace.

front: What is the Best Approximation Theorem?
back: The orthogonal projection proj_W(v) is the vector in the subspace W that is closest to v. For any w in W, ||v - proj_W(v)|| ≤ ||v - w||.

front: How is the projection matrix for projecting onto the column space of a matrix A defined?
back: If A has full column rank, the projection matrix is P = A(AᵀA)⁻¹Aᵀ. For any vector b, Pb gives the projection of b onto the column space of A (Col A).
